Quest: Maid Job_One
Autorun Event

\c[2]Clara:\c[0]
It’s the third maid that left just because you couldn’t keep your hands to yourself.

\c[4]Mr. Gilbert:\c[0]
Now, now baby, don’t accuse me without any proof.

\c[2]Clara:\c[0]
Proof! What do you think it is? Some sort of court? I know your nature very well to know why she left.

\c[4]Mr. Gilbert:\c[0]
I’m sorry, baby. It won’t happen again.

\c[2]Clara:\c[0]
No, it won’t. Because this time I’m going to hire someone you won’t be able to touch.

\c[4]Mr. Gilbert:\c[0]
Really! Who?

\c[2]Clara:\c[0]
Oh, and speaking of which, Hey Jane, finally you came to see your aunt.

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
Well, technically you are my friend’s mom. So, I am actually here to see Rebeca.

\c[2]Clara:\c[0]
Let’s not get too technical, shall we?

\c[2]Clara:\c[0]
I have known you since you were a kid when you just became friends with my daughter.

\c[2]Clara:\c[0]
So, you are like a daughter to me.

\c[2]Clara:\c[0]
But, since Julia already claimed you as her daughter, I guess will just have to be satisfied as being your aunt.

\c[2]Clara:\c[0]
You will give me that much, won’t you?

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
How can I say no, when you ask me with those puppy dog eyes?

\c[2]Clara:\c[0]
Ha-ha, puppy-dog eyes always work!

\c[2]Clara:\c[0]
But anyway, I got a text from Julia just now. Apparently, you are looking for work.

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
Yeah.

\c[2]Clara:\c[0]
Then you are in luck. Because am looking for a new maid.

\c[4]Mr. Gilbert:\c[0]
Aaha, so she is the new maid.

\c[2]Clara:\c[0]
Yes. If she agreed to take the job, that is.

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
Umm, sure. I guess.

\c[2]Clara:\c[0]
Don’t look so disappointed. No job is a small job.

\c[2]Clara:\c[0]
And I already told Gilbert to see if there is something decent for you.

\c[4]Mr. Gilbert:\c[0]
Yeah, I think one of my friends has a job available. I’ll let you know once I know for sure.

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
Thanks, for looking out for me.

\c[2]Clara:\c[0]
Of course, I am. After all, you are my favorite niece.

\c[4]Mr. Gilbert:\c[0]
Umm Ladies, I would love to spend some with you guys. But I’m getting late for work.

\c[2]Clara:\c[0]
Go then. No one is keeping you here.

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
Bye, Mr. Gilbert.

\c[2]Clara:\c[0]
So anyway, you should start working.

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
OK, what do you want me to do?

\c[2]Clara:\c[0]
First, you should change your outfit.

\c[2]Clara:\c[0]
You can wear the maid’s uniform.

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
Really? Do I have to?

\c[2]Clara:\c[0]
You don’t HAVE to. If you don’t mind getting your clothes dirty.

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
Yeah, you are right.

\c[2]Clara:\c[0]
Well then, the uniform is in that wardrobe. You can change it there.

\c[2]Clara:\c[0]
There is no one else in the house. So, it won’t be a problem.

\c[2]Clara:\c[0]
Come talk to me once you changed. I’ll let you know what to do.

\c[2]Clara:\c[0]
I will be in the kitchen.

\c[3]Jane:\c[0]
Alright.
